label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 15

extraction.one_line_summary: AARONSMEN Thymen won from 29-rider breakaway on Sierra Nevada queen stage while ROGLIC Primoz gained only 15 seconds on EVENEPOEL Remco despite repeated attacks with 7km remaining.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC waited until sub-7km on final climb to attack EVENEPOEL, suggesting concern about overextending on longer efforts at altitude.
AG2R Citroen chased 29-rider breakaway despite having O'CONNOR inside it, revealing unwillingness to cede GC positioning even to own rider.
CRADDOCK Lawson bridged solo from 29-rider break, indicating original group lacked sufficient quality climbers to deter individual ambition.
QUICK-STEP and JUMBO-VISMA planted riders in 29-man break but never activated them to pace, suggesting insurance tactics over proactive disruption.

rider_signals.AARONSMEN Thymen: positive: won queen stage from elite breakaway at age suggesting pure climbing engine can convert long-range positioning into victories
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: gained only 15 seconds despite using teammates early and attacking with 7km left, exposing limited superiority over EVENEPOEL on HC climbs
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: absorbed ROGLIC attack without teammates on Sierra Nevada final 7km, conceding minimal time despite being isolated earlier than rivals
rider_signals.RODRIGUEZ Carlos: negative: dropped position despite CARAPAZ pacing support, suggesting Stage 14 effort compromised recovery on consecutive mountain stages
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: abandoned before first categorized climb on crucial GC stage, indicating physical collapse rather than tactical withdrawal
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: attacked GC group independently on final climb, showing recovered form despite being teammate-less when LOPEZ also jumped
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: caught out of position entering final climb despite being in podium contention, revealing positioning inexperience in summit finishes
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: finished with ROGLIC despite team chasing his own breakaway, exploiting AG2R confusion to maximize personal GC without team sacrifice
